page_content:
BUSM773 Innovation & Entrepreneurship (2 Credits)

Provides an introduction to important tools and skills necessary to create and grow a successful new venture. Integrates research findings from

a range of different practical and intellectual perspectives, including psychology, sociology, economics, strategic management, and history into practical, hands on lessons for an entrepreneur. Class projects provide the foundations for new, real businesses.

Restriction: Must be in Business and Management (Master's) program; or permission of BMGT-Robert H. Smith School of Business.

Credit Only Granted for. BUMO732 or BUSM773.
